search:angular-unit-testing-best-practic.content:
- Use Angular CLI to generate test files (`ng generate component <component-name> --spec`)
- Update Angular version to the latest LTS release for best practices and security updates
- Configure Jest as the testing framework in `angular.json` under `projects.<project-name>.architect.test.options.jestConfig`
- Utilize `TestBed.configureTestingModule()` for setting up test environments
- Employ Mocks for dependencies using Jasmine's `spyOn` or Angular's `MockBuilder`
- Apply async/await with `fixture.whenStable()` for handling asynchronous operations in tests
- Use `ng-mocks` for creating mocks and stubs more efficiently
- Ensure coverage by running `ng test --code-coverage` and review results in the `coverage` folder
- Refactor tests regularly to keep them maintainable and relevant
